rwlock.c(3) Library Functions Manual rwlock.c(3)

NAME

rwlock.c - Platform-agnostic read-write lock hooks and utilities.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void rwlock_on_rdlock (rwlock_t *rwlock)
Hook called when a read lock is successfully acquired. void rwlock_on_wrlock (rwlock_t *rwlock)
Hook called when a write lock is successfully acquired. void rwlock_on_unlock (rwlock_t *rwlock)
Hook called when an rwlock is unlocked (read or write). int rwlock_format_state (const rwlock_t *rwlock, char *buffer, size_t size)
Format rwlock timing and state info into buffer. void rwlock_log_state (const rwlock_t *rwlock, const char *file, int line, const char *func)
Log the state of a read-write lock.

Detailed Description

Platform-agnostic read-write lock hooks and utilities.

This file provides hook functions that can be called from platform-specific rwlock implementations to record events and debug information. All hooks are debug-only and disabled in release builds.

Definition in file rwlock.c.

Function Documentation

int rwlock_format_state (const rwlock_t * rwlock, char * buffer, size_t size)

Format rwlock timing and state info into buffer.

Parameters

rwlock Pointer to the rwlock
buffer Output buffer
size Buffer size

Returns

Number of bytes written

Formats timing (rdlock/wrlock/unlock), held-by info, and operation counts. Called by rwlock_log_state() and --sync-state display code.

Definition at line 87 of file rwlock.c.

87                                                                            {
88   if (!rwlock || !buffer || size == 0) return 0;
89 
90   int offset = 0;
91   uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
92 
93   char rdlock_str[64] = "";
94   char wrlock_str[64] = "";
95   char unlock_str[64] = "";
96   char held_str[64] = "";
97   char count_str[256] = "";
98 
99   if (rwlock->last_rdlock_time_ns > 0 && rwlock->last_rdlock_time_ns <= now_ns) {
100     char elapsed_str[64];
101     time_pretty(now_ns - rwlock->last_rdlock_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
102     snprintf(rdlock_str, sizeof(rdlock_str), "rdlock=%s", elapsed_str);
103   }
104 
105   if (rwlock->last_wrlock_time_ns > 0 && rwlock->last_wrlock_time_ns <= now_ns) {
106     char elapsed_str[64];
107     time_pretty(now_ns - rwlock->last_wrlock_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
108     snprintf(wrlock_str, sizeof(wrlock_str), "wrlock=%s", elapsed_str);
109   }
110 
111   if (rwlock->last_unlock_time_ns > 0 && rwlock->last_unlock_time_ns <= now_ns) {
112     char elapsed_str[64];
113     time_pretty(now_ns - rwlock->last_unlock_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
114     snprintf(unlock_str, sizeof(unlock_str), "unlock=%s", elapsed_str);
115   }
116 
117   uint64_t read_count = atomic_load_u64(&rwlock->read_lock_count);
118   if (rwlock->write_held_by_key != 0) {
119     snprintf(held_str, sizeof(held_str), "[WRITE_LOCKED_BY=thread.%lu]", (unsigned long)rwlock->write_held_by_key);
120   } else if (read_count > 0) {
121     snprintf(held_str, sizeof(held_str), "[READ_LOCKED=%lu]", read_count);
122   }
123 
124   if (rwlock->rdlock_count > 0 || rwlock->wrlock_count > 0 || rwlock->unlock_count > 0) {
125     snprintf(count_str, sizeof(count_str), "[ops: rdlock=%lu wrlock=%lu unlock=%lu]",
126              rwlock->rdlock_count, rwlock->wrlock_count, rwlock->unlock_count);
127   }
128 
129   offset += snprintf(buffer + offset, size - offset, "%s %s %s %s %s",
130                      rdlock_str, wrlock_str, unlock_str, held_str, count_str);
131   return offset;
132 }




References atomic_load_u64(), rwlock,
    time_get_ns(), and time_pretty().


Referenced by rwlock_log_state().






void
  rwlock_log_state (const rwlock_t * rwlock, const char * file, int line, const
  char * func)


Log the state of a read-write lock.


Parameters


rwlock Pointer to the rwlock


file Source file of the caller


line Source line of the caller


func Source function of the caller


Formats and logs detailed state information about the rwlock.


Definition at line 145 of file rwlock.c.


145                                                                                             {
146   if (!rwlock) return;
147   char buf[512];
148   rwlock_format_state(rwlock, buf, sizeof(buf));
149   log_msg(LOG_DEBUG, file, line, func, "rwlock/state %p: %s", (const void *)rwlock, buf);
150 }






References log_msg(), rwlock, and
    rwlock_format_state().






void
  rwlock_on_rdlock (rwlock_t * rwlock)


Hook called when a read lock is successfully acquired.


Parameters


rwlock Pointer to the rwlock that
  was read-locked


Called by platform-specific rwlock_rdlock_impl() after the
    read lock is acquired. Records timing, increments current reader count and
    total rdlock count.


Definition at line 29 of file rwlock.c.


29                                         {
30   if (!rwlock) return;
31   rwlock->last_rdlock_time_ns = time_get_ns();
32   atomic_fetch_add_u64(&rwlock->read_lock_count, 1);
33   rwlock->rdlock_count++;
34 }






References atomic_fetch_add_u64(), rwlock, and
    time_get_ns().






void
  rwlock_on_unlock (rwlock_t * rwlock)


Hook called when an rwlock is unlocked (read or write).


Parameters


rwlock Pointer to the rwlock that
  was unlocked


Called by platform-specific rwlock_rdunlock_impl() or
    rwlock_wrunlock_impl() before releasing the lock. Decrements
    appropriate reader/writer count and increments total unlock count.


Definition at line 62 of file rwlock.c.


62                                         {
63   if (!rwlock) return;
64   rwlock->last_unlock_time_ns = time_get_ns();
65   rwlock->unlock_count++;
66   asciichat_thread_t current_thread = (asciichat_thread_t)asciichat_thread_current_id();
67   uintptr_t current_key = asciichat_thread_to_key(current_thread);
68   if (rwlock->write_held_by_key == current_key) {
69     rwlock->write_held_by_key = 0;
70   } else if (atomic_load_u64(&rwlock->read_lock_count) > 0) {
71     atomic_fetch_sub_u64(&rwlock->read_lock_count, 1);
72   }
73 }






References asciichat_thread_current_id(),
    atomic_fetch_sub_u64(), atomic_load_u64(), rwlock, and
    time_get_ns().






void
  rwlock_on_wrlock (rwlock_t * rwlock)


Hook called when a write lock is successfully acquired.


Parameters


rwlock Pointer to the rwlock that
  was write-locked


Called by platform-specific rwlock_wrlock_impl() after the
    write lock is acquired. Records timing, held-by thread, and increments total
    wrlock count.


Definition at line 45 of file rwlock.c.


45                                         {
46   if (!rwlock) return;
47   rwlock->last_wrlock_time_ns = time_get_ns();
48   asciichat_thread_t current_thread = (asciichat_thread_t)asciichat_thread_current_id();
49   rwlock->write_held_by_key = asciichat_thread_to_key(current_thread);
50   rwlock->wrlock_count++;
51 }






References asciichat_thread_current_id(), rwlock,
    and time_get_ns().
